name: Sarnies Bangkok
short_note: Singapore import that nails the specialty coffee and good-food formula. Reliably good flat whites.
why_good: Consistent quality where the food actually matches the coffee. Good table-to-outlet ratio.
watch_out: Popular — arrive before 9am or after 2pm to avoid queues.

name: Artis Coffee
short_note: Serious about coffee without the attitude. Clean space, quality beans, calm atmosphere for morning work sessions.
why_good: Multiple roasters on rotation. Real barista knowledge. Good table-to-outlet ratio.
watch_out: Small space. Not suited for marathon 5-hour sessions.

name: Holey Artisan Bakery
short_note: Bangkok's best bagels alongside excellent sourdough. Feels like a real bakery, not just Instagram content.
why_good: Consistently well-executed bakes. Their coffee programme is solid. Great for a working breakfast.
watch_out: Pastries sell out by mid-morning on weekends.

name: The Work Loft Silom
short_note: Clean co-working in Bangkok's business district. Consistent air-con, real desk space, and WiFi that holds a call.
why_good: Central Silom location with easy BTS access. Handles calls without the café noise problem.
watch_out: Peak hours (10am–noon) can fill up. Some days require booking ahead.

name: The Hive Thonglor
short_note: Design-forward co-working in trendy Thonglor. Comfortable and professional for longer work sessions.
why_good: Strong WiFi, ergonomic seating, and a quiet atmosphere that actually holds focus.
watch_out: Thonglor is pricier. Day pass rates add up over a full week.

name: Kajohn
short_note: Proper Thai street-food restaurant that's been around for decades. No tourist menu, no English upsell.
why_good: Pad see ew and khao man gai at prices that make you wonder why you'd eat anywhere else.
watch_out: Cash only. Very busy at lunch. Basic seating — eat fast or go off-peak.

name: GO Laundry PX
short_note: Reliable drop-off laundry service. Clean results, no drama, next-day turnaround.
why_good: Straightforward pricing, near BTS Phrom Phong. Professional handling.
watch_out: Same-day service costs extra. Drop off before noon for next-day pickup.

name: Wat Arun Viewing Point
short_note: The Wat Arun riverside view at golden hour is one of Bangkok's iconic sights — and free from the opposite bank.
why_good: The view from the Tha Tien pier side is better than being inside the temple itself.
watch_out: Very busy on weekends. Go early morning for clean photos and breathing room.

name: Mahanakhon Skywalk
short_note: Glass-floor observation deck at the top of Bangkok's tallest building. Worth doing once.
why_good: Unobstructed 360° views of Bangkok. The glass floor section is genuinely impressive at sunset.
watch_out: Pricey entry (~฿875). Skip entirely on cloudy days.
